description_long: This is a wooden half-pipe skate ramp, commonly found in urban parks or recreational areas. The structure features two curved ramps on either side, connected by a flat central platform, all bordered by wooden guardrails for safety. The ramp surface is smooth and light-colored, with visible wood grain and some colored stains or graffiti on the exterior panels. The side platforms are painted black, providing visual contrast and likely extra grip for skaters. The ramp is elevated off the ground, with sturdy wooden supports and a solid base. The overall design is functional and robust, intended for skateboarding, BMX, or scooter tricks.
description: A wooden half-pipe skate ramp with guardrails, smooth curved surfaces, and graffiti-stained exterior panels. The structure is robust and designed for skateboarding or similar activities.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat central platform, curved ramp, and a large graffiti stain on the exterior panel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full curvature of the half-pipe, black-painted side platforms, and partial graffiti on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear look at the opposite exterior panel, featuring a different colored graffiti stain.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the right-side curvature, black platform, and greenish graffiti on the lower panel.
